growth_records.lvlwhy: the source paper (often a review/modeling reuse) states no strain
growth_records.cite: Rabbers I et al. Metabolism at Evolutionary Optimal States. Metabolites (2015). DOI:10.3390/metabo5020311. [PMC4495375]
growth_records.snippet: The fastest doubling time reported in literature is for Chlostridium perfringens at only 6.3 min [92].
growth_records.notes: Review-cited maximal growth figure (primary source = ref [92], not measured in this review). Reported doubling time 6.3 min = 0.105 h; calculated mu = ln2/0.105 = 6.6 h-1. No medium/temperature/pH given.

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 2644 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/2644

phenotypes.base_medium: Enzyme / activity assay (hydrolysis, degradation, reduction, electron transfer) in a test medium — not a sole-substrate growth test
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 2644 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/2644

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 2644 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/2644
